description: US Stock Historical Events Database - covering 2022-2025 major events for top stocks and macroeconomic events

events.title: Apple Q1 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.10 vs $1.89 est. Revenue: $123.9B (+11% YoY). iPhone revenue: $71.6B, Services: $19.5B.

events.title: Apple Q2 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.52 vs $1.43 est. Revenue: $97.3B (+9% YoY). Warned of $4-8B supply chain impact.

events.title: Apple Q3 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.20 vs $1.16 est. Revenue: $83B (+2% YoY). iPhone revenue beat despite macro headwinds.

events.title: Apple Q4 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.29 vs $1.27 est. Revenue: $90.1B (+8% YoY). Record Services revenue of $19.2B.

events.title: Apple Q1 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.88 vs $1.94 est. Revenue: $117.2B (-5.5% YoY). First revenue miss since 2016.

events.title: Apple Q2 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.52 vs $1.43 est. Revenue: $94.8B (-3% YoY). iPhone sales stronger than expected.

events.title: Apple Vision Pro Announced at WWDC 2023
events.description: Apple unveiled its first spatial computer, the Vision Pro, priced at $3,499. Mixed reality headset with eye and hand tracking.

events.title: Apple Q3 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.26 vs $1.19 est. Revenue: $81.8B (-1.4% YoY). Services revenue hit record $21.2B.

events.title: iPhone 15 Launch Event
events.description: Apple announced iPhone 15 lineup with USB-C, A17 Pro chip, titanium frame for Pro models. New Apple Watch Series 9.

events.title: Apple Q4 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.46 vs $1.39 est. Revenue: $89.5B (-1% YoY). All-time high Services revenue of $22.3B.

events.title: Apple Q1 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.18 vs $2.10 est. Revenue: $119.6B (+2% YoY). iPhone revenue: $69.7B, Services: $23.1B record.

events.title: Apple Vision Pro Official Launch
events.description: Apple Vision Pro went on sale in the US at $3,499. 180,000+ pre-orders reported in first weekend.

events.title: Apple Q2 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.53 vs $1.50 est. Revenue: $90.8B (-4.3% YoY). Announced $110B buyback, largest in US history.

events.title: Apple Intelligence Announced at WWDC 2024
events.description: Apple unveiled AI strategy with 'Apple Intelligence' — generative AI features across iPhone, iPad, and Mac, including ChatGPT integration.

events.title: Apple Q3 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.40 vs $1.35 est. Revenue: $85.8B (+5% YoY). Services revenue surged to $24.2B.

events.title: iPhone 16 Launch — AI-Focused
events.description: Apple announced iPhone 16 lineup built for Apple Intelligence. New Camera Control button, A18 chips, larger displays on Pro models.

events.title: Apple Q4 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.64 vs $1.60 est. Revenue: $94.9B (+6% YoY). iPhone 16 initial sales strong. Services at record $25B.

events.title: Microsoft Q2 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.48 vs $2.31 est. Revenue: $51.7B (+20% YoY). Azure growth: +46%.

events.title: Microsoft Q3 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.22 vs $2.19 est. Revenue: $49.4B (+18% YoY). Cloud revenue: $23.4B (+32% YoY).

events.title: Microsoft Q4 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.23 vs $2.29 est. Revenue: $51.9B (+12% YoY). Weak guidance due to strong dollar.

events.title: Microsoft Q1 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.35 vs $2.30 est. Revenue: $50.1B (+11% YoY). Cloud revenue beat estimates.

events.title: Microsoft Q2 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.32 vs $2.29 est. Revenue: $52.7B (+2% YoY). Azure growth slowed to 38%.

events.title: Microsoft Announces $10B Investment in OpenAI
events.description: Microsoft announced multi-year, multi-billion dollar investment in OpenAI, deepening partnership for ChatGPT and DALL-E integration.

events.title: Microsoft Q3 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.45 vs $2.23 est. Revenue: $52.9B (+7% YoY). Azure growth: +27%. AI-driven demand boost.

events.title: Microsoft Q4 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.69 vs $2.55 est. Revenue: $56.2B (+8% YoY). AI Copilot driving growth.

events.title: Microsoft Q1 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.99 vs $2.65 est. Revenue: $56.5B (+13% YoY). Azure growth accelerated to 29%.

events.title: Sam Altman Joins Microsoft After OpenAI Ouster
events.description: After Sam Altman was fired as OpenAI CEO, Microsoft hired him to lead a new AI research team. He returned to OpenAI 5 days later.

events.title: Microsoft Q2 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.93 vs $2.78 est. Revenue: $62.0B (+18% YoY). Azure growth: +30%, 6 points from AI. Market cap crossed $3T.

events.title: Microsoft Q3 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.94 vs $2.82 est. Revenue: $61.9B (+17% YoY). Azure +31%, AI contribution accelerating.

events.title: Microsoft Q4 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.95 vs $2.93 est. Revenue: $64.7B (+15% YoY). Azure +33%. AI revenue run rate: $5B+.

events.title: Microsoft Q1 FY2025 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $3.30 vs $3.10 est. Revenue: $65.6B (+16% YoY). AI Copilot subscriptions doubling quarterly.

events.title: Alphabet Q4 2021 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $30.69 vs $27.35 est. Revenue: $75.3B (+32% YoY). Announced 20-for-1 stock split.

events.title: Alphabet Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $24.62 vs $25.91 est. Revenue: $68B (+23% YoY). YouTube ad revenue missed estimates.

events.title: Alphabet 20-for-1 Stock Split
events.description: Google parent Alphabet executed a 20-for-1 stock split, reducing share price from ~$2,200 to ~$110.

events.title: Alphabet Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.21 vs $1.28 est. Revenue: $69.7B (+13% YoY). Ad slowdown fears confirmed.

events.title: Alphabet Q3 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.06 vs $1.25 est. Revenue: $69.1B (+6% YoY). YouTube ad revenue declined for first time.

events.title: Alphabet Q4 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.05 vs $1.18 est. Revenue: $76B (+1% YoY). Miss across Search, YouTube, and Cloud.

events.title: Google Announces Bard AI (predecessor to Gemini)
events.description: Google announced Bard, its conversational AI service to compete with ChatGPT. Early demo had a factual error.

events.title: Alphabet Q1 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.17 vs $1.07 est. Revenue: $69.8B (+3% YoY). Announced $70B buyback. Cloud profitable for first time.

events.title: Alphabet Q2 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.44 vs $1.34 est. Revenue: $74.6B (+7% YoY). Cloud revenue +28%. Ad rebound confirmed.

events.title: Alphabet Q3 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.55 vs $1.45 est. Revenue: $76.7B (+11% YoY). Cloud revenue missed expectations.

events.title: Google Launches Gemini AI Model
events.description: Google launched Gemini, its most capable AI model with multimodal capabilities. Available in Ultra, Pro, and Nano sizes.

events.title: Alphabet Q4 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.64 vs $1.59 est. Revenue: $86.3B (+13% YoY). Ad revenue: $65.5B. Cloud: $9.2B.

events.title: Alphabet Q1 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.89 vs $1.51 est. Revenue: $80.5B (+15% YoY). First-ever dividend ($0.20/share) + $70B buyback.

events.title: Alphabet Q2 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.89 vs $1.84 est. Revenue: $84.7B (+14% YoY). Cloud surpassed $10B quarterly for first time.

events.title: Alphabet Q3 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.12 vs $1.85 est. Revenue: $88.3B (+15% YoY). All segments beat. Cloud: $11.4B.

events.title: Amazon Q4 2021 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $27.85 vs $3.66 est (Rivian investment gain). Revenue: $137.4B (+9% YoY). AWS revenue: $17.8B.

events.title: Amazon Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$7.56 (Rivian writedown). Revenue: $116.4B (+7% YoY). AWS: $18.4B (+37% YoY).

events.title: Amazon 20-for-1 Stock Split
events.description: Amazon executed a 20-for-1 stock split, its first since 1999, bringing share price from ~$2,500 to ~$125.

events.title: Amazon Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$0.20 vs $0.14 est. Revenue: $121.2B (+7% YoY). AWS: $19.7B (+33%). Strong Q3 guidance.

events.title: Amazon Q4 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.03 vs $0.17 est. Revenue: $149.2B (+9% YoY). AWS growth slowed to 20%.

events.title: Amazon Q1 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.31 vs $0.15 est. Revenue: $127.4B (+9% YoY). AWS growth stabilized at 16%.

events.title: Amazon Q2 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.65 vs $0.35 est. Revenue: $134.4B (+11% YoY). AWS growth reaccelerated to 12%.

events.title: Amazon Q3 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.94 vs $0.58 est. Revenue: $143.1B (+13% YoY). AWS +12%. Advertising: $12.1B.

events.title: Amazon Q4 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.00 vs $0.80 est. Revenue: $170B (+14% YoY). AWS +13%. Record holiday quarter.

events.title: Amazon Q1 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.98 vs $0.83 est. Revenue: $143.3B (+13% YoY). AWS growth accelerated to +17%. AI demand surging.

events.title: Amazon Q2 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.26 vs $1.03 est. Revenue: $148B (+10% YoY). AWS +19%. Ad revenue: $12.8B.

events.title: Amazon Q3 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.43 vs $1.14 est. Revenue: $158.9B (+11% YoY). AWS +20%. Operating income tripled YoY.

events.title: NVIDIA Q4 FY2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.32 vs $1.22 est. Revenue: $7.64B (+53% YoY). Data center: $3.26B, Gaming: $3.42B.

events.title: NVIDIA Q1 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.36 vs $1.29 est. Revenue: $8.29B (+46% YoY). Data center revenue surpassed gaming for first time.

events.title: NVIDIA Q2 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.51 vs $1.25 est. Revenue: $6.7B (+3% YoY). Gaming revenue crashed 33%. Preliminary warning was issued 2 weeks prior.

events.title: NVIDIA Q3 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.58 vs $0.71 est. Revenue: $5.93B (-17% YoY). Gaming: -51%. Data center: +31%. Crypto mining collapse impact.

events.title: NVIDIA Q4 FY2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.88 vs $0.81 est. Revenue: $6.05B (-21% YoY). Data center: $3.62B, AI driving recovery.

events.title: NVIDIA Q1 FY2024 Earnings — AI Boom Begins
events.description: EPS: $1.09 vs $0.92 est. Revenue: $7.19B. Q2 guidance: $11B (vs $7.2B est). 'New computing era' — Jensen Huang.

events.title: NVIDIA Q2 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.70 vs $2.09 est. Revenue: $13.51B (+101% YoY). Data center: $10.3B. Q3 guidance: $16B.

events.title: NVIDIA Q3 FY2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $4.02 vs $3.37 est. Revenue: $18.12B (+206% YoY). Warned China export restrictions would impact Q4.

events.title: NVIDIA Q4 FY2024 Earnings — Historic Quarter
events.description: EPS: $5.16 vs $4.64 est. Revenue: $22.1B (+265% YoY). Data center: $18.4B. 'AI has hit the tipping point'.

events.title: NVIDIA GTC 2024 — Blackwell Platform Unveiled
events.description: Jensen Huang unveiled Blackwell B200 GPU, the 'world's most powerful chip' with 208B transistors. 4x faster than H100 for AI training.

events.title: NVIDIA Q1 FY2025 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $6.12 vs $5.59 est. Revenue: $26.0B (+262% YoY). 10-for-1 stock split announced. Dividend raised 150%.

events.title: NVIDIA 10-for-1 Stock Split
events.description: NVIDIA executed 10-for-1 stock split, bringing share price from ~$1,200 to ~$120. Market cap at $3T.

events.title: NVIDIA Q2 FY2025 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.68 vs $0.64 est. Revenue: $30.0B (+122% YoY). Blackwell delay concerns addressed. $50B buyback.

events.title: NVIDIA Q3 FY2025 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.81 vs $0.75 est. Revenue: $35.1B (+94% YoY). Blackwell ramp on track. Data center demand still supply-constrained.

events.title: Tesla Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $3.22 vs $2.26 est. Revenue: $18.8B (+81% YoY). Record deliveries: 310k vehicles.

events.title: Tesla Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.27 vs $1.81 est. Revenue: $16.9B (+42% YoY). Bitcoin impairment: $170M. Sold 75% of BTC holdings.

events.title: Tesla 2022 Shareholder Meeting — 3-for-1 Stock Split
events.description: Tesla shareholders approved a 3-for-1 stock split. Elon Musk reaffirmed full self-driving timeline.

events.title: Tesla Q3 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.05 vs $0.99 est. Revenue: $21.5B (+56% YoY). Missed delivery expectations. Musk: 'recession concerns'.

events.title: Elon Musk Completes Twitter Acquisition
events.description: Elon Musk completed his $44B acquisition of Twitter, taking the company private. Concerns about his divided attention.

events.title: Tesla Q4 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.19 vs $1.13 est. Revenue: $24.3B (+37% YoY). Aggressive price cuts announced. 2023 delivery guidance: 1.8M.

events.title: Tesla Q1 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.85 vs $0.85 est. Revenue: $23.3B (+24% YoY). Margins compressed to 19.3% (vs 29.1% a year ago).

events.title: Tesla Q2 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.91 vs $0.82 est. Revenue: $24.9B (+47% YoY). Record deliveries: 466k. Cybertruck: 'first deliveries in 2023'.

events.title: Tesla Q3 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.66 vs $0.73 est. Revenue: $23.4B (+9% YoY). Missed on both. Margins hit 17.9%. Cybertruck launch Nov 30.

events.title: Cybertruck Delivery Event
events.description: Tesla delivered first Cybertrucks at Austin Gigafactory. Base price $60,990 (higher than initially promised $39,900).

events.title: Tesla Q4 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.71 vs $0.74 est. Revenue: $25.2B (+3% YoY). Warned of 'notably lower' 2024 volume growth. No guidance number.

events.title: Tesla Q1 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.45 vs $0.51 est. Revenue: $21.3B (-9% YoY, first decline since 2020). Announced layoffs of 10%+ workforce.

events.title: Tesla Q2 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.52 vs $0.62 est. Revenue: $25.5B (+2% YoY). Robotaxi event pushed to Oct 10. Energy storage at record.

events.title: Tesla 'We Robot' Robotaxi Event
events.description: Tesla unveiled Cybercab robotaxi (no steering wheel/pedals, <$30k), Robovan, and Optimus robots. Production 'before 2027'.

events.title: Tesla Q3 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $0.72 vs $0.58 est. Revenue: $25.2B (+8% YoY). Profit beat on lower costs. 2025 delivery growth: 20-30%.

events.title: Meta Q4 2021 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $3.67 vs $3.84 est. Revenue: $33.7B (+20% YoY). DAU declined for first time. Weak Q1 guidance. Zuckerberg: 'focus on Reels and metaverse'.

events.title: Meta Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.72 vs $2.56 est. Revenue: $27.9B (+7% YoY). DAU rebounded. Metaverse losses: $2.96B.

events.title: Meta Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.46 vs $2.59 est. Revenue: $28.8B (-1% YoY, first decline). Warned of 'economic downturn'.

events.title: Meta Q3 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.64 vs $1.89 est. Revenue: $27.7B (-4% YoY). CapEx guidance raised. Metaverse losses accelerating.

events.title: Meta Announces 11,000 Layoffs — First Mass Layoff
events.description: Mark Zuckerberg announced 11,000 job cuts (~13% of workforce), first mass layoff in company history. 'Year of Efficiency' begins.

events.title: Meta Q4 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $1.76 vs $2.22 est. Revenue: $32.2B (-4% YoY). Announced $40B buyback. Expense cuts positive.

events.title: Meta Announces 10,000 More Layoffs
events.description: Meta announced another 10,000 layoffs and 5,000 open role closures as part of 'Year of Efficiency'. Total cuts: 21,000.

events.title: Meta Q1 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.20 vs $2.03 est. Revenue: $28.6B (+3% YoY). First revenue growth in 4 quarters. AI ad tools working.

events.title: Threads Launch — 'Twitter Killer'
events.description: Meta launched Threads, its Twitter/X competitor. Reached 100M users in 5 days, fastest-growing app in history.

events.title: Meta Q2 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.98 vs $2.91 est. Revenue: $32.0B (+11% YoY). Q3 guidance above estimates. Reels monetization improving.

events.title: Meta Q3 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $4.39 vs $3.63 est. Revenue: $34.1B (+23% YoY). Ad revenue surging. Reality Labs losses: $3.7B.

events.title: Meta Q4 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $5.33 vs $4.96 est. Revenue: $40.1B (+25% YoY). First-ever dividend: $0.50/share. $50B buyback expansion.

events.title: Meta Q1 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $4.71 vs $4.32 est. Revenue: $36.5B (+27% YoY). AI spending plans spooked market. Capex raised to $35-40B.

events.title: Meta Q2 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $5.16 vs $4.73 est. Revenue: $39.1B (+22% YoY). AI spending now 'a good thing long-term'. Llama 3.1 open-sourced.

events.title: Meta Q3 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $6.03 vs $5.25 est. Revenue: $40.6B (+19% YoY). AI driving 20%+ ad revenue growth. Reality Labs still burning $4.4B.

events.title: JPMorgan Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.63 vs $2.72 est. Revenue: $30.7B (-5% YoY). Investment banking fees -31%. Built $902M credit reserves.

events.title: JPMorgan Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.76 vs $2.88 est. Revenue: $30.7B (+1% YoY). Suspended share buybacks. 'Storm clouds on horizon' — Dimon.

events.title: JPMorgan Q3 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $3.12 vs $2.88 est. Revenue: $32.7B (+10% YoY). NII boosted by rising rates. Trading revenue +8%.

events.title: JPMorgan Q4 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $3.57 vs $3.07 est. Revenue: $34.5B (+18% YoY). Record NII. Warned of mild recession.

events.title: JPMorgan Acquires First Republic Bank in Crisis
events.description: After SVB and Signature Bank failures, JPMorgan led $30B rescue package for First Republic. Later acquired it outright in May.

events.title: JPMorgan Acquires First Republic Bank
events.description: JPMorgan Chase acquired most of First Republic Bank for $10.6B in a government-led deal after its collapse. Gained $203B in assets.

events.title: JPMorgan Q2 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $4.37 vs $4.00 est. Revenue: $41.3B (+34% YoY). First Republic boost + higher rates driving record NII.

events.title: JPMorgan Q3 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $4.33 vs $3.89 est. Revenue: $39.9B (+22% YoY). Jamie Dimon: 'This may be the most dangerous time the world has seen in decades.'

events.title: Johnson & Johnson Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.67 vs $2.59 est. Revenue: $23.4B (+5% YoY). Medical devices: +8%. Consumer health: -1.5%.

events.title: Johnson & Johnson Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.59 vs $2.54 est. Revenue: $24.0B (+3.9% YoY). Raised full-year guidance despite currency headwinds.

events.title: Johnson & Johnson Q3 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.55 vs $2.48 est. Revenue: $23.8B (+1.9% YoY). Announced Kenvue consumer health spin-off plans.

events.title: Johnson & Johnson Q1 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: $2.68 vs $2.50 est. Revenue: $24.7B (+5.6% YoY). Raised 2023 guidance. Kenvue IPO planned.

events.title: Kenvue (J&J Consumer Health) IPO
events.description: Johnson & Johnson spun off consumer health unit Kenvue in largest US IPO since Rivian (2021). Raised $3.8B at $41B valuation.

events.title: Boeing Q4 2021 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$7.69 vs -$0.41 est. Revenue: $14.8B (-3% YoY). 787 delivery delays: $4.5B charge. But positive free cash flow for first time since 2019.

events.title: Boeing Q1 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$2.75 vs -$0.27 est. Revenue: $14.0B (-8% YoY). 777X delay to 2025. $1.5B in charges.

events.title: Boeing Q2 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$0.37 vs -$0.14 est. Revenue: $16.7B (-2% YoY). 787 deliveries resumed after 15-month pause.

events.title: Boeing Q3 2022 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$6.18 vs $0.13 est. Revenue: $16.0B (+4% YoY). Massive charges on defense programs: $2.8B.

events.title: Boeing Q1 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$1.27 vs -$1.07 est. Revenue: $17.9B (+28% YoY). 737 MAX deliveries ramping. Free cash flow improving.

events.title: Boeing Q2 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$0.82 vs -$0.88 est. Revenue: $19.8B (+18% YoY). Commercial deliveries: 136 aircraft. $3B free cash flow.

events.title: Boeing Q3 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$3.26 vs -$2.96 est. Revenue: $18.1B (+13% YoY). 737 delivery target cut due to fuselage defect.

events.title: Alaska Airlines 737 MAX 9 Door Plug Blowout
events.description: An Alaska Airlines 737 MAX 9 suffered a mid-air door plug blowout at 16,000 feet. FAA grounded all 737 MAX 9 aircraft with door plugs.

events.title: Boeing Q4 2023 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$0.47 vs -$0.78 est. Revenue: $22.0B (+10% YoY). Withdrew 2024 guidance due to MAX 9 crisis. 'Earn trust back.'

events.title: Boeing CEO Dave Calhoun to Step Down
events.description: Boeing announced CEO Dave Calhoun would step down by end of 2024, along with Chairman and Commercial Airplanes head, in sweeping leadership shakeup.

events.title: Boeing Q2 2024 Earnings
events.description: EPS: -$2.90 vs -$1.91 est. Revenue: $16.9B (-15% YoY). $1.4B loss. New CEO Kelly Ortberg appointed.

events.title: Fed Signals March Rate Hike in Minutes
events.description: FOMC minutes from December meeting showed Fed officials discussed accelerating taper and earlier/faster rate hikes, surprising dovish-leaning markets.

events.title: January CPI: 7.5% YoY (40-Year High)
events.description: US CPI rose 7.5% YoY in January, the highest since February 1982. Core CPI: 6.0%. Both well above estimates.

events.title: Russia Invades Ukraine
events.description: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, triggering global market turmoil. Oil surged above $100/barrel for first time since 2014.

events.title: FOMC: First Rate Hike Since 2018 — +25bp to 0.25-0.50%
events.description: Fed raised rates by 25bp, first hike since 2018. Dot plot projected 7 hikes in 2022. Powell: 'Inflation is much too high.'

events.title: FOMC: +50bp Rate Hike — Largest in 22 Years
events.description: Fed raised rates 50bp to 0.75-1.00%. Powell ruled out 75bp hikes. Announced balance sheet runoff starting June 1 at $47.5B/month.

events.title: May CPI: 8.6% YoY — New 40-Year High
events.description: US CPI surged to 8.6% YoY, exceeding the March high. Gasoline, shelter, and food drove the increase. Markets priced in more aggressive Fed.

events.title: FOMC: +75bp — Largest Hike Since 1994
events.description: Fed raised rates 75bp to 1.50-1.75% after CPI surprise. Powell: 'Today's 75bp increase is unusually large.' Dot plot: 3.4% by year-end.

events.title: FOMC: +75bp Hike to 2.25-2.50%
events.description: Fed raised rates 75bp again. Powell: 'Another unusually large increase could be appropriate.' But noted growth slowing. Markets rallied on perceived dovish tone.

events.title: Jackson Hole: Powell's Hawkish 8-Minute Speech
events.description: Powell's brief but forceful Jackson Hole speech: 'Reducing inflation will bring some pain.' Markets tumbled on hawkish message.

events.title: August CPI: 8.3% YoY (Above Est)
events.description: August CPI came in at 8.3% YoY vs 8.1% expected. Core CPI rose 0.6% MoM (double expectations). Worst single day of 2022.

events.title: FOMC: +75bp to 3.00-3.25% — Dot Plot Shocker
events.description: Fed hiked 75bp for third straight time. Dot plot showed 4.6% terminal rate (vs 3.8% in June). Powell: 'We need to see inflation coming down.'

events.title: FOMC: +75bp to 3.75-4.00% — Dovish Signals
events.description: Fed hiked 75bp (fourth straight) but signaled slower pace ahead. Powell: 'Time to slow pace of hikes may come as soon as December.'

events.title: October CPI: 7.7% YoY (Below Est) — Peak Inflation Narrative
events.description: October CPI came in below expectations at 7.7% (vs 7.9% est). Core CPI: 6.3% vs 6.5% est. Best single day for equities since 2020.

events.title: FOMC: +50bp to 4.25-4.50% — Slowdown Confirmed
events.description: Fed downshifted to 50bp hike. Dot plot: terminal rate 5.1%. Powell: 'We have more work to do.' Hawkish messaging despite smaller hike.

events.title: FOMC: +25bp to 4.50-4.75% — 'Disinflation Has Begun'
events.description: Fed hiked 25bp, further slowing pace. Powell acknowledged: 'The disinflationary process has begun.' Dovish tone sent markets higher.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank Collapse
events.description: SVB, the 16th largest US bank, collapsed after a bank run — the largest US bank failure since 2008. Triggered regional banking crisis.

events.title: UBS Acquires Credit Suisse in Emergency Deal
events.description: UBS agreed to buy Credit Suisse for CHF 3 billion in a government-brokered emergency takeover. Swiss regulator wrote down CHF 16B of AT1 bonds to zero.

events.title: FOMC: +25bp to 4.75-5.00% — Banking Crisis Dovishness
events.description: Despite banking crisis, Fed hiked 25bp. Powell: 'Rate cuts are not in our base case.' But dot plot still showed 5.1% terminal. Markets priced in cuts by year-end.

events.title: FOMC: +25bp to 5.00-5.25% — Hints at Pause
events.description: Fed hiked 25bp for potentially the last time in this cycle. Removed language about 'additional policy firming'. Markets bet on July rate cut.

events.title: Debt Ceiling Deal Signed into Law
events.description: President Biden signed the Fiscal Responsibility Act, suspending the debt ceiling until January 2025, averting a catastrophic US default.

events.title: FOMC: +25bp to 5.25-5.50% — 'Data Dependent'
events.description: Fed hiked 25bp after June pause. Powell: 'We're going to make decisions meeting by meeting.' Core inflation at 4.8%.

events.title: Hamas Attacks Israel — Middle East Conflict Escalates
events.description: Hamas launched a massive attack on Israel, killing 1,200+. Israel declared war. Oil prices surged on geopolitical risk.

events.title: October CPI: 3.2% YoY (Below Est) — Soft Landing Hope
events.description: October CPI came in at 3.2% vs 3.3% est, the first downside surprise in months. Core CPI: 4.0% vs 4.1% est. Rate cut expectations surged.

events.title: FOMC: Hold at 5.25-5.50% — Dovish Pivot — Rate Cuts Coming
events.description: Fed held rates steady. Dot plot showed 75bp of cuts in 2024 (up from 50bp in September). Powell: 'We're likely at or near the peak rate.' Markets priced in 150bp of cuts.

events.title: FOMC: Hold at 5.25-5.50% — March Cut 'Probably Not'
events.description: Fed held rates. Powell: 'We want to see more good data.' Explicitly said March rate cut unlikely. Pushed back on market pricing.

events.title: March CPI: 3.5% YoY (Third Hot Print In a Row)
events.description: CPI rose 3.5% YoY vs 3.4% est. Third consecutive hot inflation print. Markets repriced: first cut pushed from June to September.

events.title: July Jobs Report Triggers 'Sahm Rule' Recession Signal
events.description: US added only 114K jobs vs 175K est. Unemployment rose to 4.3%, triggering the Sahm Rule recession indicator. Market panic.

events.title: Global Market Rout — Japan Carry Trade Unwind
events.description: Nikkei crashed -12.4% (worst since 1987 Black Monday). Yen carry trade unwind triggered global margin calls. S&P intraday -4.3%. VIX hit 65.

events.title: FOMC: -50bp Jumbo Cut to 4.75-5.00% — New Easing Cycle Begins
events.description: Fed started easing cycle with outsized 50bp cut (first cut since 2020). Powell: 'We're committed to maintaining economy's strength.' Projected 100bp more cuts by year-end 2025.

events.title: Trump Wins 2024 Presidential Election — GOP Sweep
events.description: Donald Trump won the 2024 presidential election with a decisive victory. Republicans also won Senate and held House, delivering a 'red sweep'.

events.title: FOMC: -25bp to 4.50-4.75% — Deja Vu
events.description: Fed cut 25bp as expected. Powell said election would have no near-term effect on policy and refused to say whether he would resign if Trump asked.

events.title: FOMC: -25bp to 4.25-4.50% — Hawkish Cut — Fewer 2025 Cuts
events.description: Fed cut 25bp but slashed 2025 rate cut projections from 4 to 2 cuts. Powell: 'We're in a new phase.' Inflation still above target. Markets sold off hard.
